lecture_text: The same organism used in bread and beer production. A model organism for eukaryotic cell biology in laboratories. Does not cause disease in healthy humans and does not spread between people.
feedback_correct: Correct! BSL-1 is sufficient — basic lab coat and gloves when needed.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Baker's yeast is completely harmless to healthy adults. BSL-1 is the only level required.

lecture_text: A common soil bacterium widely used in molecular biology research. Not pathogenic to healthy humans. Used as a model in sporulation research.
feedback_correct: Correct! BSL-1 is sufficient — no infection risk for healthy individuals.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Bacillus subtilis is a BSL-1 organism — it does not cause disease.

lecture_text: A genetically weakened laboratory version of E. coli. Unable to colonize the human intestine. The most commonly used research bacterium in molecular biology worldwide.
feedback_correct: Correct! Laboratory E. coli is BSL-1 — a weakened strain with no infection risk.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Laboratory strains are specifically weakened. BSL-1 is sufficient.

lecture_text: A yeast found in dairy products, used in protein production. Does not cause disease. A GRAS organism (generally regarded as safe).
feedback_correct: Correct! BSL-1 is sufficient for this safe yeast.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Kluyveromyces lactis is a BSL-1 fungus — completely safe.

lecture_text: A virus used as a gene therapy vector. Cannot replicate without a helper virus. In its natural form, does not cause disease in humans. Used in medical gene therapy.
feedback_correct: Correct! AAV is BSL-1 — a gene vector that does not cause illness.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! AAV is a safe gene vector, BSL-1 is sufficient.

lecture_text: A bacterium that is part of the normal gut microbiome, found in yogurt and probiotic products. A beneficial organism, not a pathogen. No infection risk.
feedback_correct: Correct! Lactobacillus is a BSL-1 probiotic organism.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! This lactic acid bacterium is part of normal gut flora — BSL-1 is sufficient.

lecture_text: An aquatic bacterium used as a model organism in cell division research. Not pathogenic to humans. A common research bacterium in cell biology.
feedback_correct: Correct! Caulobacter crescentus is BSL-1.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Caulobacter does not cause disease — BSL-1 is the correct level.

lecture_text: An archaeon living in extremely salty water. Used as a model for photosynthesis and membrane research. Cannot survive in human body conditions.
feedback_correct: Correct! Halobacterium is a BSL-1 extremophile.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Halobacterium salinarum cannot survive in our body's conditions. BSL-1 is sufficient.

lecture_text: A photosynthetic cyanobacterium used in solar energy research. Not pathogenic. A model organism for photosynthesis research.
feedback_correct: Correct! Synechocystis is a BSL-1 cyanobacterium.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! This cyanobacterium is BSL-1 — a photosynthetic non-pathogen.

lecture_text: A virus that infects Bacillus subtilis bacteria, used in DNA replication research. Cannot infect human cells. Used in biotechnology.
feedback_correct: Correct! The bacteriophage is BSL-1 — does not infect human cells.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Bacteriophages are BSL-1 viruses — they do not infect humans.

lecture_text: A bacterium common in soil and plant roots. A non-pathogenic species of the Pseudomonas genus. Used in bioremediation and research.
feedback_correct: Correct! P. fluorescens is BSL-1 — a non-pathogenic soil species.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Pseudomonas fluorescens is BSL-1, unlike its pathogenic relatives.

lecture_text: A soil species used as a gene transfer vector in plant biology. Does not infect humans. A common tool in plant genetic engineering.
feedback_correct: Correct! Agrobacterium is BSL-1 — it only infects plants.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Agrobacterium does not infect humans — BSL-1 is sufficient.

lecture_text: A unicellular organism living in water, used in cell biology research. Not pathogenic. A research model that has earned Nobel Prizes (telomeres, ribozymes).
feedback_correct: Correct! Tetrahymena is a BSL-1 model organism.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Tetrahymena does not cause disease — a BSL-1 research model.

lecture_text: A virus that infects E. coli bacteria, used for decades in fundamental molecular biology research. Cannot infect human cells. A classic research model for genetic regulation.
feedback_correct: Correct! The lambda phage is BSL-1.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! The lambda phage only infects E. coli bacteria — BSL-1 is sufficient.

lecture_text: A red mold fungus used in genetics research since the 1940s. A research model that has led to Nobel Prizes. Not pathogenic to healthy humans.
feedback_correct: Correct! Neurospora crassa is a BSL-1 model fungus.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Neurospora crassa is a classic genetics research model — BSL-1 is sufficient.

lecture_text: An annual seasonal influenza virus that spreads via droplets and air. Causes fever, muscle aches, and respiratory symptoms. Vaccine available. Most severe for the elderly and risk groups.
feedback_correct: Correct! Seasonal influenza A is BSL-2.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Seasonal flu is a BSL-2 virus — zoonotic strains such as H5N1 are BSL-3.

lecture_text: One of the most contagious human viruses — spreads via airborne droplets and aerosols. Causes rash, fever, and can lead to pneumonia. An effective MMR vaccine provides protection.
feedback_correct: Correct! Measles virus is BSL-2 — vaccine provides protection.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Measles virus is BSL-2 because an effective vaccine is available.

lecture_text: Work with the immunodeficiency virus. The virus does not spread through the air — infection requires direct blood contact. Treatment prevents disease progression.
feedback_correct: Correct! HIV is BSL-3 — classified as Group 3 under Finnish regulation 748/2020.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! HIV is classified as BSL-3 in Finland (regulation 748/2020), even though infection requires direct blood contact.

lecture_text: A highly contagious bloodborne virus. Spreads through blood, sex, and mother-to-child contact. Can cause chronic liver inflammation and cirrhosis. A vaccine is available.
feedback_correct: Correct! Hepatitis B is BSL-3 — classified as Group 3 under Finnish regulation 748/2020.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Hepatitis B is classified as BSL-3 in Finland (regulation 748/2020), even though transmission requires blood contact.

lecture_text: The leading cause of liver transplants worldwide. Spreads through blood contact. No vaccine, but current antiviral treatment is highly effective.
feedback_correct: Correct! Hepatitis C is BSL-3 — classified as Group 3 under Finnish regulation 748/2020.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Hepatitis C is classified as BSL-3 in Finland (regulation 748/2020).

lecture_text: The most common cause of food poisoning. Spreads through contaminated food or water. Causes abdominal symptoms, diarrhea, and fever. Does not spread through the air in laboratories.
feedback_correct: Correct! Salmonella is BSL-2 — foodborne, not airborne.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Salmonella spreads through food — BSL-2 is sufficient.

lecture_text: A common bacterium found on human skin. Can cause wound infections, pneumonia, or sepsis. MRSA strains are antibiotic-resistant. Spreads through contact.
feedback_correct: Correct! Staphylococcus aureus is BSL-2.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Staph is a BSL-2 bacterium — not airborne.

lecture_text: A bacterium spread by ticks. Causes a bullseye rash, joint pain, and neurological symptoms. Does not spread person to person. Antibiotic treatment is effective in early stages.
feedback_correct: Correct! Borrelia burgdorferi is BSL-2 — tick-borne, not person-to-person.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Borrelia does not spread between people — BSL-2 is sufficient.

lecture_text: A bacterium spread through contaminated water. Causes massive watery diarrhea. Does not spread through the air. A vaccine is available.
feedback_correct: Correct! Cholera is BSL-2 — waterborne, not airborne.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Vibrio cholerae is BSL-2 — spreads through water.

lecture_text: Spreads through the air and by contact. Causes itchy blisters. Reactivation of the same virus causes shingles in adults. A vaccine is available.
feedback_correct: Correct! VZV is BSL-2 — vaccine and treatment available.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Chickenpox virus is BSL-2 — an effective vaccine exists.

lecture_text: The most common cause of acute gastroenteritis. Highly contagious — only a few viral particles cause infection. Spreads through feces and vomit. No effective treatment or vaccine.
feedback_correct: Correct! Norovirus is BSL-2 — spreads via the fecal-oral route.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Norovirus is BSL-2 — not airborne.

lecture_text: A sexually transmitted bacterium. Progresses in stages: painless sore, rash, internal organ damage. Does not spread through laboratory air. Penicillin remains an effective treatment.
feedback_correct: Correct! Treponema pallidum is BSL-2 — not airborne.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Syphilis is BSL-2 — transmission requires direct mucosal contact.

lecture_text: A bacterium spread through droplets. Causes severe coughing fits, especially dangerous for infants. Vaccine provides effective protection.
feedback_correct: Correct! Bordetella pertussis is BSL-2 — vaccine provides protection.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Whooping cough is BSL-2 — a vaccine is available.

lecture_text: The most common sexually transmitted bacterial infection. Often asymptomatic. Left untreated, can cause infertility. Antibiotic treatment is effective.
feedback_correct: Correct! Chlamydia trachomatis is BSL-2.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Chlamydia is BSL-2 — easily treatable.

lecture_text: A dangerous cause of meningitis in young people. Spreads through droplets. Can lead to death within hours. Vaccine protects against the most important serotypes.
feedback_correct: Correct! Neisseria meningitidis is BSL-2 — vaccine provides protection.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Meningococcus is BSL-2 — a vaccine is available.

lecture_text: Cats are the primary host. Infection occurs through contaminated meat or cat feces. Dangerous for pregnant women. Does not spread person to person.
feedback_correct: Correct! Toxoplasma gondii is BSL-2.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Toxoplasma is a BSL-2 parasite — not transmitted person to person.

lecture_text: A waterborne parasite. Causes severe diarrhea, especially in immunocompromised individuals. Oocysts resist chlorination. Found in contaminated drinking water.
feedback_correct: Correct! Cryptosporidium parvum is BSL-2.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Cryptosporidium is a BSL-2 parasite.

lecture_text: The most common cause of human fungal infections. Normally lives on mucous membranes, can cause severe infection in immunocompromised individuals. Not airborne. Allergenic.
feedback_correct: Correct! Candida albicans is a BSL-2 fungus.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Candida is BSL-2 — not transmitted person to person.

lecture_text: A common environmental fungus. Spores are inhaled daily. Can cause severe lung infection in immunosuppressed individuals. Allergenic. Not transmitted person to person.
feedback_correct: Correct! Aspergillus fumigatus is BSL-2.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Aspergillus is BSL-2 — not transmitted person to person.

lecture_text: A foodborne bacterium that can grow at refrigerator temperatures. Especially dangerous for pregnant women, newborns, and immunocompromised individuals. Not airborne.
feedback_correct: Correct! Listeria monocytogenes is BSL-2.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Listeria is BSL-2 — foodborne.

lecture_text: A mosquito-borne flavivirus found in tropical regions. Causes high fever, joint pain, and rash. In severe form, hemorrhage is possible. Not transmitted directly person to person.
feedback_correct: Correct! Dengue is BSL-3 — classified as Group 3 under Finnish regulation 748/2020, with no exception noted.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Dengue is classified as BSL-3 in Finland (regulation 748/2020) with no special exception.

lecture_text: The most common sexually transmitted virus. Certain strains can cause cervical cancer. Vaccine protects against the most important cancer-causing strains.
feedback_correct: Correct! HPV is BSL-2 — vaccine provides protection.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! HPV is BSL-2 — a vaccine is available.

lecture_text: Liver inflammation spread via the fecal-oral route. Common in developing countries from contaminated water or food. No chronic form. Vaccine provides effective protection.
feedback_correct: Correct! Hepatitis A is BSL-2 — vaccine provides protection.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Hepatitis A is BSL-2 — spreads via the fecal-oral route.

lecture_text: A bacterium that enters wounds from soil. Produces a nerve toxin that causes muscle spasms. The vaccine is highly effective.
feedback_correct: Correct! Clostridium tetani is BSL-2 — vaccine provides protection.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Tetanus is a BSL-2 bacterium — the vaccine is highly effective.

lecture_text: Less severe malaria species than P. falciparum. Spreads through mosquitoes, not person to person in laboratories. Treatment is available.
feedback_correct: Correct! Plasmodium species other than falciparum are BSL-2.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Malaria species other than P. falciparum are BSL-2.

lecture_text: The world's second leading cause of infectious disease death. Spreads through the air via small aerosol particles. Can remain latent for decades. Multidrug-resistant strains are a growing threat.
feedback_correct: Correct! TB is BSL-3 — spreads through the air, requires a respirator and full-body suit.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! TB is a very serious airborne bacterium. BSL-3 is required: respirator, full-body suit, double gloves.

lecture_text: A spore-forming bacterium that can survive in soil for decades. Respiratory exposure causes a severe pulmonary form. Has also been used as a bioweapon.
feedback_correct: Correct! Bacillus anthracis is BSL-3 — spores can spread through the air.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Anthrax is BSL-3 — respiratory exposure is extremely dangerous.

lecture_text: Virus culture work with the COVID-19 virus, which spreads through the air as aerosols. Culturing the live virus is far more hazardous than diagnostic testing alone.
feedback_correct: Correct! Virus culture of SARS-CoV-2 requires BSL-3 protection.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Virus culture of SARS-CoV-2 requires BSL-3 — the virus spreads through the air as aerosols.

lecture_text: The bacterium that caused the Black Death. Spreads via fleas, but the pneumonic form is airborne. Antibiotic treatment is effective today.
feedback_correct: Correct! Plague is BSL-3 — the pneumonic form spreads through the air.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Yersinia pestis is BSL-3 — the pneumonic form in particular is very dangerous.

lecture_text: A hantavirus spread by bank voles, Finland's most common hemorrhagic fever. Spreads as an aerosol from rodent secretions. Can cause severe kidney failure.
feedback_correct: Correct! Puumala orthohantavirus (wild-type strain) is BSL-3.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! This hantavirus is BSL-3 — aerosol transmission requires respiratory protection.

lecture_text: A tropical mosquito-borne flavivirus. Causes high fever and liver damage. An effective vaccine exists.
feedback_correct: Correct! Yellow fever virus is BSL-3.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Yellow fever virus is BSL-3 — the severe clinical picture requires BSL-3 protection.

lecture_text: A flavivirus spread by ticks, found in coastal areas of Finland. Causes a two-phase illness: first flu-like symptoms, then encephalitis. A vaccine is available.
feedback_correct: Correct! TBE virus is BSL-3.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! The cause of tick-borne encephalitis is BSL-3 — risk of brain inflammation.

lecture_text: The most dangerous malaria parasite. Spreads through mosquitoes. Causes cerebral malaria, a life-threatening complication.
feedback_correct: Correct! Plasmodium falciparum is BSL-3.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! P. falciparum is the most dangerous malaria parasite — BSL-3 is required.

lecture_text: A bacterium transmitted from animals to humans. Can spread as aerosols. Causes prolonged fever, joint pain, and organ damage.
feedback_correct: Correct! Brucella melitensis is BSL-3 — aerosol transmission possible.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Brucella is BSL-3 — can spread as aerosols.

lecture_text: A chlamydia transmitted through bird droppings. Causes severe pneumonia. Particular risk for bird breeders and veterinarians. Aerosol transmission possible.
feedback_correct: Correct! The avian strain of Chlamydia psittaci is BSL-3 — aerosol risk.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! The cause of parrot fever is BSL-3 — aerosol transmission makes it more dangerous.

lecture_text: One of the most dangerous viruses in the world. Causes hemorrhagic fever. Spreads through direct contact with body fluids. No effective treatment.
feedback_correct: Correct! Ebola is BSL-4 — requires a positive-pressure suit with its own air supply.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Ebola is BSL-4 — a positive-pressure suit with its own air supply is mandatory.

lecture_text: A filovirus closely related to Ebola. Causes severe hemorrhagic fever. No treatment.
feedback_correct: Correct! Marburg virus is BSL-4.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Marburg is BSL-4 — a full protective suit is essential.

lecture_text: A West African arenavirus. Spreads from rodents and person to person. Can cause deafness as an aftereffect. No effective treatment.
feedback_correct: Correct! Lassa virus is BSL-4.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Lassa virus is BSL-4 — spreads person to person without effective treatment.

lecture_text: A paramyxovirus carried by bats. Spreads person to person. Extremely high mortality. No vaccine or specific treatment.
feedback_correct: Correct! Nipah virus is BSL-4 — extremely high mortality.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Nipah is BSL-4 — spreads person to person, no treatment.

lecture_text: An Australian paramyxovirus carried by bats. Transmitted from horses to humans. Closely related to Nipah virus. No treatment.
feedback_correct: Correct! Hendra virus is BSL-4.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Hendra is BSL-4 — no treatment.

lecture_text: A nairovirus spread by ticks. Also spreads through direct blood contact. Found in Africa, Asia, and Southern Europe. No effective treatment.
feedback_correct: Correct! CCHFV is BSL-4.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Crimean-Congo hemorrhagic fever virus is BSL-4.

lecture_text: Eradicated from nature in 1980. Today it is stored at only two high-security laboratories in the world.
feedback_correct: Correct! Variola virus is BSL-4.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Variola major is a BSL-4 pathogen — eradicated from nature.

lecture_text: A Bolivian arenavirus spread by rodents. Has also spread person to person in hospital settings. No effective treatment.
feedback_correct: Correct! Machupo virus is BSL-4.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Machupo is BSL-4 — severe hemorrhagic fever without treatment.

lecture_text: An Argentine arenavirus. Spreads from rodents to humans. Only specialized laboratories are permitted to study it.
feedback_correct: Correct! Junín virus is BSL-4.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! Junín is a BSL-4 pathogen — severe hemorrhagic fever.

lecture_text: A rare but always fatal prion disease of the brain. Prions are not destroyed by standard sterilization. The incubation period can be decades.
feedback_correct: Correct! The CJD prion is BSL-3.
feedback_incorrect: Incorrect! The Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease prion is BSL-3 — not destroyed by standard sterilization.
